verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is the beta subunit of mitochondrial F1Fo ATP synthase, canonically resident on the matrix face of the inner mitochondrial membrane. However, ectopic cell-surface expression of ATP synthase components (including the beta subunit) is well documented on tumor cells, hepatocytes, endothelial cells, and adipocytes, where surface F1-ATPase serves as a receptor for apolipoproteins, angiostatin, and other ligands. This cancer/tissue-restricted ecto-ATP synthase pool has been targeted by antibodies and small molecules. Ruling out other buckets: not a lysosomal TM protein; not held by wash-resistant covalent TM-partner tethering; surface display is the cell-state/tissue-induced ecto-form, best captured by cell_state_induced.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Mechanism of ecto-ATP synthase translocation from mitochondria to PM remains unclear; surface pool is real but minor relative to mitochondrial pool.
